name: Unenroll member by MemberId
description: Documented Sample JSON Response (doc lines 23161-23177): member successfully unenrolled, returning the MemberId in outputParameters.outputParameters.results. Request uses the MemberId variant of processParameters (doc lines 23142-23149).
notes: SOURCE: official Loyalty Management Developer Guide v67.0 (Summer 26). The UnenrollMember endpoint has no dedicated resource block; it is one of the named processes under the generic "Loyalty Program Process" resource (doc lines 22538-22656). That generic block is where HTTP method (POST, line 22603), Available version (56.0, line 22599), the processParameters request wrapper (Loyalty Process Parameter Map, lines 22623-22629), and the response schema (Loyalty Process Output, line 22633) are defined. The UnenrollMember-specific "Unenroll a Member" section (doc lines 23121-23181) supplies the sample request JSON (MemberId variant + CurrentMembershipNumber variant) and the sample success response. The response property table is the "Loyalty Process" schema (doc lines 29969-29988): message (String, may be null), outputParameters (Map<String,Object>), simulationDetails (Map<String,Object>, doc notes "This property is for future use", so it is empty {}), status (Boolean true/false). IMPLEMENTER-DEFINED: doc explicitly states (lines 23129-23130) "The request and response parameters vary depending on the input and output parameters configured for the UnenrollMember process type in a Salesforce org." So the inner field names inside processParameters[] and inside outputParameters.outputParameters.results[] (e.g. MemberId, CurrentMembershipNumber) are org-configured/process-template-configured, NOT fixed by the API. The doc also warns that some request field names are interchangeable (see "Loyalty Management Connect API Interchangeable Request Fields"). PATH NOTE: canonical resource template is /connect/loyalty/programs/${programName}/program-processes/${processName}; for this process the processName segment is the literal "UnenrollMember" (per resource-examples table, doc lines 22584-22586). The doc's prose "Example URL" wraps the word across lines as "unenroll Member" but that is a PDF line-wrap artifact of "UnenrollMember", not a space in the path. OPEN QUESTIONS: doc shows no documented error/failure-status example for this specific process, so no error fixture was fabricated; on failure the standard envelope would set status=false with a message string, but that exact shape is not shown in the doc for UnenrollMember. NOTE on doc index quirk: the appendix resource list (line 42498) mislabels UnenrollMember's description as "Opt Out from a Promotion", that is a doc error; the authoritative description from the definition (line 22585 / 23122) is "Unenroll a member from a loyalty program."
